title: Tapinanthus redingii
taxonomy: Tapinanthus redingii is classified as a species of parasitic flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae. This family is a group of hemiparasitic plants that obtain some of their nutrients by connecting to the roots of host plants. Tapinanthus redingii is a type of mistletoe, which is a group of plants known for their unique parasitic lifestyle. The Loranthaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions, including Africa and Asia. Tapinanthus redingii is one of the many species within this family, which is known for its diverse range of species and distribution.
